reasoning: This kernel performs 2 FLOPs per element (one multiply, one add) while transferring 12 bytes per element (two float reads + one float write), yielding an arithmetic intensity of ~0.17 FLOP/byte — far below the A10G ridge point of 62 FLOP/byte. With n = 2^26 elements and fully coalesced access patterns across 4096×256 threads, the kernel will saturate DRAM bandwidth while leaving compute units vastly underutilized. This is a classic bandwidth-limited elementwise operation where DRAM utilization will be high relative to peak memory bandwidth.
